title: CV Explorations
text:
This is a repo-as-curriculum: each major computer-vision capability is a self-contained module that is both runnable and explained. Every module ships a reusable CLI/engine, an interactive notebook, and a rigorous benchmark report — multiple models per task, real metrics, latency measured on the local GPU, and honest failure cases.
The reports you’re reading were rendered from results computed locally on an NVIDIA RTX 3090 Ti (24 GB); heavier or Linux-only work runs on an NVIDIA DGX Spark over Tailscale. Site rendering itself needs no GPU.

(pre-deep-learning)

⏳ planned

title: CV Explorations
text:
A useful mental model
The biggest shift in modern CV is task-specific models → promptable foundation models:

Old way: train one model per task on a fixed label set.
New way: one model you prompt — SAM (“segment whatever I point at”), Grounding DINO (“detect anything I name in text”), CLIP (“classify against any labels”), Florence-2 (“detect, caption, OCR, and ground from one checkpoint”).

Each module shows both, side by side, so the why of the modern approach is visible rather than asserted.

title: CV Explorations
text:
How to run it yourself
uv sync --group dev                # core env (PyTorch + CV stack)
uv sync --group data --group detection   # extras for the detection module
uv run python modules/01-object-detection/run.py --help
See the repository README for the full setup and hardware notes.
